items.title: Celebrations and mourning in Tehran after strikes kill Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

items.title: 3/1: Face The Nation
items.standfirst: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran continue into a second day after the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Senate Intelligence Committee Chairman Tom Cotton joins, as well as Sens. Chris Murphy and Ted Cruz.

items.title: Trump says Iran leadership agrees to talks after US and Israel strike Tehran
items.standfirst: ‘They should have done it sooner, they waited too long,’ says Trump but he doesn’t say when talks would take placeUS-Israel war on Iran – latest updatesSign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump said on Sunday that Iran’s political leadership have agreed to talks, a day after the US and Israel began to target the country’s military and political infrastructure, killing the supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and several top officials.“They want to talk, and I have agreed to talk, so I will be talking to them,” Trump told a reporter for the Atlantic magazine on Sunday. “They should have done it sooner. They should have given what was very practical and easy to do sooner. They waited too long.” Continue reading...
items.content: ‘They should have done it sooner, they waited too long,’ says Trump but he doesn’t say when talks would take placeUS-Israel war on Iran – latest updatesSign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump said on Sunday that Iran’s political leadership have agreed to talks, a day after the US and Israel began to target the country’s military and political infrastructure, killing the supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and several top officials.“They want to talk, and I have agreed to talk, so I will be talking to them,” Trump told a reporter for the Atlantic magazine on Sunday. “They should have done it sooner. items.title: Dubai influencers stunned by Iranian strikes
items.standfirst: Influencers gone to Dubai for its luxurious lifestyle are now watching, helpless and sometimes shocked, as Iran attacks the Gulf countries, retaliating against the large-scale Israeli-American air strikes operation that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Al Khamenei. Since Saturday, many expatriates in the United Arab Emirates have been sharing videos of plumes of smoke rising above skyscrapers.

items.title: UK to allow US to use British bases for defensive strikes against Iran
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer says decision is due to Iran’s approach becoming more reckless and putting British lives at riskThe UK has agreed to let the US use British military bases to attack Iranian missile sites, Keir Starmer has said.The UK has so far not been involved in the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, but in a recorded statement on Sunday evening, the prime minister said that Iran’s approach was becoming more reckless and putting British lives at risk, leading to the decision to allow the US to use two of its military bases. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Deadly Texas bar shooting is 'potentially an act of terrorism', FBI says
clusters.neutral_headline: Deadly Texas bar shooting is 'potentially an act of terrorism', FBI says
bullet_summary: But he added that evidence found on the suspect and in his car indicated a “potential nexus to terrorism”
At least two people were killed and 14 were injured in a mass shooting outside an Austin, Texas, bar, authorities said Sunday

items.title: Deadly Texas bar shooting is 'potentially an act of terrorism', FBI says
items.standfirst: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.
items.content: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.

items.title: Austin bar shooting leaves three dead, including suspect, and 14 wounded
items.standfirst: FBI official says evidence found on the suspect and in his car indicated a ‘potential nexus to terrorism’Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe FBI’s joint terrorism taskforce has been called in to help investigate a deadly mass shooting in downtown Austin, Texas, on Sunday morning in which a gunman opened fire in front of a bar popular with university students, killing two people and injuring 14 others before being fatally shot by police.An FBI official, Alex Doran, told reporters at a press conference that it was too early to determine the shooter’s motivation. But he added that evidence found on the suspect and in his car indicated a “potential nexus to terrorism”. Continue reading...
items.content: FBI official says evidence found on the suspect and in his car indicated a ‘potential nexus to terrorism’Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe FBI’s joint terrorism taskforce has been called in to help investigate a deadly mass shooting in downtown Austin, Texas, on Sunday morning in which a gunman opened fire in front of a bar popular with university students, killing two people and injuring 14 others before being fatally shot by police.An FBI official, Alex Doran, told reporters at a press conference that it was too early to determine the shooter’s motivation. But he added that evidence found on the suspect and in his car indicated a “potential nexus to terrorism”. Continue reading...

items.title: 2 dead, 14 hurt in Texas bar shooting, FBI investigating possible terrorism links
items.standfirst: At least two people were killed and 14 were injured in a mass shooting outside an Austin, Texas, bar, authorities said Sunday. There were indicators on the suspect and in his vehicle that suggest a "potential nexus to terrorism," but it's too early to determine the motivation, FBI Acting Special Agent Alex Doran said during a briefing Sunday. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.
items.content: At least two people were killed and 14 were injured in a mass shooting outside an Austin, Texas, bar, authorities said Sunday. There were indicators on the suspect and in his vehicle that suggest a "potential nexus to terrorism," but it's too early to determine the motivation, FBI Acting Special Agent Alex Doran said during a briefing Sunday. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

clusters.title: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
clusters.neutral_headline: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
bullet_summary: Footage from local media showed people rushing to collect banknotes while police in riot gear tried to disperse them using teargas
Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
items.standfirst: Police fire tear gas to disperse crowds allegedly trying to take banknotes from the crash site.
items.content: Police fire tear gas to disperse crowds allegedly trying to take banknotes from the crash site.

items.title: At least 20 killed as cash-laden military cargo plane crashes in Bolivia
items.standfirst: Riot police use teargas to disperse people gathering around wreckage of plane loaded with money from central bankAt least 20 people have died and dozens have been injured after a military cargo plane carrying banknotes crashed while landing near Bolivia’s capital on Friday, damaging about a dozen vehicles on a highway and scattering bills on the ground, an official has said.Footage from local media showed people rushing to collect banknotes while police in riot gear tried to disperse them using teargas. Authorities were later seen setting the money alight in a bonfire at the scene of the crash. Continue reading...
items.content: Riot police use teargas to disperse people gathering around wreckage of plane loaded with money from central bankAt least 20 people have died and dozens have been injured after a military cargo plane carrying banknotes crashed while landing near Bolivia’s capital on Friday, damaging about a dozen vehicles on a highway and scattering bills on the ground, an official has said.Footage from local media showed people rushing to collect banknotes while police in riot gear tried to disperse them using teargas. Authorities were later seen setting the money alight in a bonfire at the scene of the crash. Continue reading...
